heading: Create the updateMonthlySales Trigger
body:
| UI Field Name        | Configuration                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Trigger Type         | Select Scheduled.                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| Name                 | Specify updateMonthlySales.                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Schedule Type        | Select Basic.For Repeat once by, select Day of the Month and set the value to your preferred date.Alternatively, for testing purposes, set Repeat once by dropdown to a more frequent occurrence, such as Minute or Hour. |
| Select An Event Type | Select Function.                                                                                                                                                                                                          |

The function for this trigger defines a
`monthlyPhoneTransactions` materialized view that contains cumulative
monthly sales information. The function updates monthly sales
information for sales conducted over the phone.

Paste the following code into the function:

```javascript
exports = function(){

   var pipeline = [
     { $match: {purchaseMethod: "Phone"} },
     { $unwind: {path: "$items"}},
     { $group: {
      _id: { $dateToString:
      { format: "%Y-%m", date: "$saleDate" } },
      sales_quantity: { $sum: "$items.quantity"},
      sales_price: { $sum: "$items.price"}
     }},
     { $set: { sales_price: { $toDouble: "$sales_price"}}},
     { $merge: { into: "monthlyPhoneTransactions", whenMatched: "replace" } }
   ]

   var monthlyPhoneTransactions = context.services.get("mongodb-atlas").db("sample_supplies").collection("sales");

   return monthlyPhoneTransactions.aggregate(pipeline);
};
```

The function uses the following aggregation pipeline stages to update
`monthlyPhoneTransactions`:

- The [$match](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/match) stage filters the data to process only those
  sales that were completed over the `Phone`.

- The [$group](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/group) stage groups the sales information by the
  year-month. This stage outputs documents of the form:

  ```javascript
  { "_id" : "<YYYY-mm>", "sales_quantity" : <num>, "sales_amount" : <NumberDecimal> }
  ```

- The [$set](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/set) stage changes the data type of the `sales_price`
  field to `double`. MongoDB Search `$search` operators don't support the
  `Decimal128` data type. Changing the `sales_price` field's data
  type allows you to query this field using MongoDB Search indexes.

- The [$merge](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) stage writes the output to the
  `monthlyPhoneTransactions` collection.

  [Based](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) on the `_id` field, the stage checks if the
  document in the aggregation results [match](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge)
  an existing document in the collection:

  - When MongoDB Search finds a [match](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) (that is, a
    document with the same year-month already exists in the collection),
    MongoDB Search [replaces](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) the existing
    document with the document from the aggregation results as specified
    in the stage.

  - When MongoDB Search doesn't find a [match](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge), MongoDB Search
    inserts the document from the aggregation results into the collection
    as specified in the stage.

Click the Run button in the
lower right-hand corner of the Function Editor
to create the `monthlyPhoneTransactions` materialized view.

The Result tab at the bottom of the
Function Editor reflects the execution status of
the function. Click Save Draft.
